rules: Use 'Space Mono' for all text to maintain an authentic but cheerful terminal feel.
Never use generic fonts like Inter or Roboto.

layouts.global: Full screen (h-screen w-screen overflow-hidden) wrapper holding a horizontal Swiper component.
layouts.section: Each slide contains a centered Terminal Window container (w-full max-w-4xl h-[85vh] md:h-[80vh] mx-4 md:mx-auto flex flex-col).
layouts.terminal_window: Rounded corners (rounded-xl), crisp 1px border (border-slate-200), subtle drop shadow (shadow-2xl shadow-slate-200/50), top bar with macOS style 3 dots, inner content area with padding and overflow-y-auto.

components.terminal_wrapper: A reusable component for each section. Props: title (e.g., 'dain@portfolio:~/about'). It should render the macOS title bar and the scrolling body.
components.typing_text: Use react-type-animation or a custom Framer Motion staggered text setup for the sequential typing effect of the content from top to bottom.
components.navigation: Persistent small dot indicators (Swiper pagination) at the bottom. Also include a subtle 'Swipe left/right' hint on the home screen.
components.contact_form: Terminal styled form. Inputs should have transparent backgrounds (bg-transparent), subtle bottom borders instead of full boxes, and monospace text. Hide default focus rings, use custom colored rings.

motion.content_reveal: Typing effect from top to bottom inside the terminal. Delay the rendering of buttons/forms until the text finishes typing if possible.
motion.swipe_hint: Blinking or pulsing horizontal arrows at the bottom of the Home terminal.

accessibility: Keyboard arrow keys must be enabled in Swiper to navigate between sections.
Contrast ratios must be high (charcoal text on white/light-gray).
Include data-testid on all interactive elements (e.g., data-testid='contact-submit-button').
UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: DO NOT use generic centered layouts. Emphasize the macOS terminal window aesthetic.
DO NOT use dark mode. Follow the bright/cheerful terminal user choice.
Use JS (jsx) not TS (tsx) for all files. Do not generate TypeScript.
Add transition for interactive elements (hover states on buttons/links).
DO NOT use transparent backgrounds for the main terminal body. It must be solid white to ensure text readability.
For the background outside the terminal, consider a very subtle CSS dot-pattern grid to add tech texture.
Use the accents (pink, cyan, amber, indigo) to color-code different terminal outputs (e.g., pink for user input, cyan for commands, amber for warnings).

Home.content: Terminal greeting. Types out a welcome message, Dain's name, and his title 'Cybersecurity & AI Enthusiast'.
Home.special: Must include a visible, animated swipe gesture hint or arrows.

About.content: Terminal displaying bio about Dain Ramnauth. Passions: building AI models, network security, Linux operating systems.

Skills.content: Terminal displaying skills output. Groups: AI/ML Learning, Linux (ParrotOS, Kali Linux), Security Tools (Aircrack-ng, Wireshark), Concepts (Penetration testing, WiFi security). Can be styled as a JSON object or an ASCII table.

Projects.content: Terminal output listing 2 projects: 1. GitScout (GitHub repo search with rate-limit handling). 2. ClipBridge (Real-time cross-device clipboard sharing with room codes). Include interactive links/buttons.

Contact.content: Terminal interactive prompt for Name, Email, and Message. Sends POST request to /api/contact.

avatar.description: Minimalist cheerful developer avatar. Can be used in the About section as ASCII art or rendered as an image with terminal border.
